tax: Placed at genus rank, Stormling is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a striking charged-cloud body. A new species belongs here only if it shows a lightning-striking charged body.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a dark charged cloud 2 to 3 m across, crawling with blue-white arcs and veined with living discharge. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — arc-scorch, fused stone, an ozone tang, and hair-raising charge that builds before each strike — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Stormling occupies charged storm-shafts. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where air-pressure charges a storm pocket and recur with the shaft re-charges, sustaining themselves on atmospheric charge.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: strikes grounded metal and wet crews, builds to a stunning discharge, and crawls arcs along metal to reach distant prey. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: a charged cloud body, electrical-field sensing, electricity-immunity, and a defining grounding-failure. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each species and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: branching lightning and stunning discharge. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Stormling matters because lightning hazard near metal. It lets survey reports sort striking-cloud forms from clinging-mote ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Stormling is the genus-rank grouping of lightning-striking storm-cloud elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a charged cloud body that strikes with branching lightning, and divide below into species groups by charge build and strike range. A living storm-cloud that hurls lightning; the only Air genus that strikes at range.
